claims_verified.claim: kickoff.sh captures a non-flag token after --new as BRIEF and excludes it from REST
claims_verified.evidence: scripts/kickoff.sh:63-85; manual bash trace with ARGS=(--new --skip-clarify --reason hi) produced BRIEF=[] REST=--skip-clarify --reason hi

claims_verified.claim: --brief is forwarded only to the --phase new invocation, never to clarify/design/loop
claims_verified.evidence: scripts/kickoff.sh:102 (`--phase new ${BRIEF:+--brief "$BRIEF"}`) vs lines 108/114/122 which expand only ${REST[@]+...}; tests/test_kickoff_wiring.py:47-64 test_kickoff_forwards_brief_only_to_new asserts this statically

claims_verified.claim: ${BRIEF:+--brief "$BRIEF"} preserves spaces in the brief as one token and expands to nothing when BRIEF is empty
claims_verified.evidence: manual bash test: BRIEF="hello world with spaces" -> argv [--brief][hello world with spaces] (2 tokens); BRIEF="" -> ARGC=2, no --brief token emitted

claims_verified.claim: empty brief (--brief "") behaves as no brief in _phase_new via a falsy check
claims_verified.evidence: orchestrator/run.py:212 `seed = ... if brief else ...`; tests/test_run_cli.py test_new_mode_empty_brief_same_as_no_brief asserts seed == '# freshtask\n' with --brief ""

claims_verified.claim: no-brief authoring prompt is byte-identical to the pre-change SPEC_AUTHOR_PROMPT
claims_verified.evidence: orchestrator/run.py:99-106 appends SPEC_AUTHOR_BRIEF_BLOCK only `if brief`; tests/test_run_cli.py test_default_author_spec_no_brief_is_byte_identical asserts captured prompt == SPEC_AUTHOR_PROMPT.format(...)

claims_verified.claim: Deps.author_spec signature and every call site/test were updated consistently to the 4-arg (wt, task_id, spec_rel, brief) shape
claims_verified.evidence: grep -rn author_spec across orchestrator/run.py and tests/test_run_cli.py: all lambdas/functions use 4 params; no stale 3-arg caller remains

claims_verified.claim: full local gate passes on this branch
claims_verified.evidence: ruff check . -> 'All checks passed!'; basedpyright -> '0 errors, 2133 warnings, 0 notes'; pytest -n auto -q -> '1113 passed, 9 skipped, 3 warnings in 71.23s'

test_assessment: AC1 (interactive session with brief in prompt, no clarify crash): covered by test_default_author_spec_threads_brief_into_prompt plus the kickoff.sh brief-only-to-new routing test and manual trace confirming --brief never reaches REST/clarify. AC2 (no-brief byte-identical): covered by test_default_author_spec_no_brief_is_byte_identical and test_new_mode_no_brief_seed_and_prompt_unchanged. AC3 (seed contains brief; empty-authoring guard fires against brief-inclusive seed): covered by test_new_mode_with_brief_seeds_spec_and_threads_it and test_new_mode_empty_authoring_guard_fires_with_brief. AC4 (empty brief == no brief): covered for run.py by test_new_mode_empty_brief_same_as_no_brief; for kickoff.sh the ${BRIEF:+...} empty-expansion path has no repo-level test, only my manual shell trace during review (residual risk, not a blocker given it's a one-line, well-understood shell idiom already used elsewhere in the same file for REST). AC5 (flag-looking token after --new not swallowed as brief): protected by the `[[ "${ARGS[$i]}" != -* ]]` guard in kickoff.sh but has no dedicated automated test; verified manually. AC6 (unit tests for routing, seeding/threading, no-brief regression, empty-guard-with-brief): fully satisfied by the new tests in tests/test_kickoff_wiring.py and tests/test_run_cli.py. Both new/changed functions (_default_author_spec, _phase_new) and the kickoff.sh parsing loop have at least one exercising test, including the error path (rc==2) with and without a brief.
residual_risks: kickoff.sh's --new capture logic and the empty-BRIEF expansion are verified only by static text assertions plus manual bash tracing during this review, not by a test that actually executes kickoff.sh end-to-end; a future shell refactor could regress this silently.
AC5 (flag-looking token after --new treated as a separate flag) has no dedicated repo test.
